PFE.reasoning: PFE broke down through the $26.68 pivot support (tested 29 times) on April 28 with volume 58.08M vs 20-day avg 34.0M — 1.71x average, which meets the volume confirmation requirement. That was the breakdown day. Current price $26.26 is now trading below both key pivot lows ($26.68 tested 29x and $26.36 tested 12x), confirming a bearish range breakdown. The consolidation range was roughly $26.68–$27.67 (resistance), giving a range height of ~$1.00; measured-move target below $26.68 = ~$25.68. However, confidence is capped at 42 because: (1) we are post-breakdown on day 2, meaning the ideal short entry window (on the break day or a backtest of the broken level) has passed; (2) daily RSI 7 is at an extreme 17.19 — deeply oversold, with a bounce already underway on the hourly chart (hourly price $26.70 vs daily close $26.26, hourly RSI 21 = 47.94, hourly ADX plus_di 27.18 > minus_di 13.59); (3) daily BB width is moderate at 0.0821 — no classic squeeze setup preceded this breakdown; (4) daily volume today is only 0.88x average — no fresh confirmation. Wiki cross-check: the wiki had flagged $26.68 support as BofA floor (originally $26), and noted that a break below $26 opens a short toward $24–25. The BofA $26 floor has not yet been decisively broken (daily close $26.26 still above it). Best entry for a bearish play would be a bounce to the $26.68 broken support level that fails with volume. Do not chase here into oversold conditions.

XOM.reasoning: XOM formed a short-term 3-day consolidation range from approximately April 24–28 between lows near $147.97 and highs near $152.31 (range height ~$4.34). April 29 produced a breakout candle closing at $154.67, above the consolidation high. However, this breakout FAILS the volume requirement: today's volume was 14.67M vs the 20-day average of 19.91M — only 0.74x average, well below the required 1.5x minimum. Without volume confirmation, strategy rules classify this as a suspect / false breakout candidate. Additional concerns: (1) daily BB width is 0.1409 — already wide/expanded, NOT a squeeze setup, meaning volatility expansion is already underway rather than coiling for a break; (2) hourly OBV is diverging bearishly from price — hourly price making new highs but OBV trending down; (3) hourly RSI 21 at 73.48 is in overbought territory; (4) the nearest daily resistance is $155.89 (tested 11 times) — only $1.22 above current price, limiting upside before the next obstacle; (5) the Fib 61.8% retracement level at $155.13 sits just above. Wiki cross-check: prior runs through April 17 consistently carried a bearish bias with short entries at exactly this $151–$155 zone. The invalidation for the prior bearish thesis was a $160+ sustained close — we are NOT there yet at $154.67. The daily ADX still shows minus_di (29.86) > plus_di (22.10), meaning the longer-term bearish directional trend has not yet reversed. The 5-day momentum bounce is real but unconfirmed by volume. Waiting for either: (a) a close above $155.89 with 1.5x+ volume to confirm a bullish breakout, or (b) a rejection from $155–156 that returns to the range — which would be a confirmed failed breakout short entry.

PFE.reasoning: PFE presents a genuine battleground between the downtrend (trend_momentum, breakout both bearish) and an extreme oversold condition (mean_reversion bullish at RSI-7 17.19). The apparent 2-1 bearish vote overstates conviction: trend_momentum confidence is only 55 (capped explicitly because of RSI extreme and in-progress hourly bounce), breakout is 42 (past the ideal entry window, no fresh confirmation), and neither agent wants to chase here — both are waiting for a pullback bounce to 26.55-26.85 to enter short. Catalyst context is decisive: Q1 2026 earnings print on May 5 is 4 trading days out. Catalyst_news scored only 38 confidence and explicitly ruled the pre-catalyst setup non-constructive. Macro_context passed at 30 confidence due to sub-2:1 R/R and direct Section 232 pharmaceutical tariff headwinds that cannot be offset by the broad risk-on macro. The mean_reversion bullish case (RSI-7 17.19, price at lower Bollinger Band, hourly OBV recovering) is real but the downtrend ADX structure (-DI 28.87 vs +DI 15.72) warns that oversold can remain oversold. With earnings 4 days out, any directional swing position carries binary event risk on top of the unresolved trend-vs-reversion conflict. The correct call is to hold — wait for the May 5 earnings reaction to clarify the next directional leg before initiating a new swing position in either direction.
PFE.key_conflicts: The core conflict is trend_momentum (bearish, 55) vs mean_reversion (bullish, 62). Trend_momentum sees ADX=25.48 with -DI 28.87 dominating +DI 15.72 and a negative ROC cascade (-2.01%/-3.42%/-5.44%) as confirmation of a live downtrend. Mean_reversion sees RSI-7 at 17.19 — extreme oversold — plus a lower-Bollinger Band tag and hourly OBV recovery as a genuine snapback setup. This disagreement tells us the downtrend is extended and reversal risk is rising, but not yet confirmed. Breakout (bearish, 42) agrees directionally with trend_momentum but is cautious because the breakdown happened two days ago (no fresh entry), daily RSI is at 17.19, and the ideal backtest-of-broken-level entry has not yet materialized. Catalyst_news (neutral) and macro_context (neutral) both independently veto a directional entry: the earnings binary on May 5 adds unquantifiable event risk, and the Section 232 pharma tariff headwind makes the downside structural while the R/R math falls below the 2:1 minimum for a long entry. Two strategies want to short on a bounce, one wants to buy the oversold extreme, two say wait — that is not a consensus worth trading into.

XOM.reasoning: XOM has only one directional signal across five agents: macro_context bullish (58) driven by the Iran military blockade / oil price catalyst. The other four agents are split or neutral: mean_reversion is bearish (52) on the overbought RSI-7 79.95 and Fib 61.8% resistance at $155.13, while trend_momentum is neutral (30, no clean EMA alignment despite daily close above all short-term MAs), breakout is neutral (28, volume only 0.74x on the breakout candle — far below 1.5x requirement), and catalyst_news is neutral (42, earnings in 1 trading day is a binary event). The macro veto framework applies in both directions here: macro_context is bullish but explicitly warns against chasing at $154.67 and requires a pullback to $153 before entry. With Q1 earnings printing on May 1 (tomorrow), no other strategy agent provides directional support, and hourly OBV diverging bearishly from price at an overbought RSI reading, a neutral hold is correct. The macro_context bullish thesis (Iran blockade + oil spike) is legitimate and could become a high-conviction trade AFTER the earnings binary is resolved. The mean_reversion short (target $147.80) is technically sound but entering a short into an event-driven macro setup with genuine oil supply risk is dangerous. The productive disagreement between macro_context (bullish on geopolitical oil catalyst) and mean_reversion (bearish on RSI/Fib exhaustion) reflects a genuine regime-vs-technicals tension: if the Iran blockade escalates and oil spikes, technical resistance dissolves; if the catalyst deflates post-earnings, mean_reversion wins. The setup resolves post-earnings.
XOM.key_conflicts: The dominant conflict is macro_context (bullish, 58) vs mean_reversion (bearish, 52). Macro_context sees the Iran military blockade as a direct oil-price catalyst for XOM with R/R of 2.9:1 from entry $153 to target $159.61 (stop $150.68) — a legitimate macro-driven setup. Mean_reversion sees RSI-7 at 79.95, price directly at the Fib 61.8% retracement ($155.13), prior wiki entries identifying this exact $151-155 zone as a short target, and bearish hourly OBV divergence — a textbook overbought fade. This disagreement is informative: the technicals say extended and exhausted; the macro says a supply-shock catalyst can override technical resistance. Three additional agents reinforce caution rather than either direction: trend_momentum (neutral) notes daily MACD still negative and -DI still leads +DI at the daily level, confirming the daily trend has NOT reversed; breakout (neutral) flags the unconfirmed volume on the breakout candle (0.74x vs 1.5x required) suggesting a potential bull trap; and catalyst_news (neutral) notes that Q1 earnings in 1 day carry complex offsetting forces (upstream beat vs downstream miss) with analyst targets already at current price. The aggregate picture: one agent bullish on a macro catalyst, one bearish on technical exhaustion, three neutral with event-risk caution — a 3-of-5 neutral hold is the disciplined response ahead of the May 1 earnings print.

PFE.reasoning: TREND: EMAs are tangled (10 EMA 26.83, 21 EMA 27.04, 50 EMA 26.96 — not cleanly stacked), but price (26.26) is below every EMA including the 50, and on the hourly chart EMAs are aligned downtrend. XOM.reasoning: TREND: EMA alignment is tangled — ema_10 (151.05) < ema_50 (151.37) < ema_21 (152.67), not in clean uptrend order. `ema_aligned_uptrend: false` and `ema_aligned_downtrend: false` confirmed. Despite price trading above all short-term EMAs at 154.67, the ADX structure tells the bearish story: ADX=31.17 (strong trend) with -DI (29.86) > +DI (22.10), meaning the dominant trend force is still bearish. PFE.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on recovery (S&P 500 at ATH, best April since 2020 at +9% MTD), but higher-for-longer rates (Fed held 3.5-3.75% with 8-4 dissent) and Iran geopolitical tail risk dominate the structural picture. Crucially, the Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs (100% on patented drugs signed April 2, 2026) create a direct regulatory headwind for PFE that macro risk-on does not offset. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: PFE is materially underperforming the market. ROC 5d=-2.01%, 10d=-3.42%, 21d=-5.44% vs S&P +9% in April. PFE is below ALL daily EMAs (EMA5 through EMA50) while the market makes all-time highs. ADX=25.48 with minus_di=28.87 >> plus_di=15.72 confirms a strong ongoing downtrend. OBV trending down, MACD in bearish territory. This is a textbook sector laggard — no sector inflows, only outflows. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Current daily close ~$26.26, hourly ~$26.70. Nearest resistance at $27.67 (first daily pivot high). Upside from hourly price to resistance: ~3.6%. Downside from $26.70 to Bollinger lower/recent low cluster at $26.12-$26.20: ~1.9%. Asymmetry ~1.9:1 — below the 2:1 minimum bar for entry. Fib extension targets exist at $29.44 (1.272x) but require a full trend reversal unsupported by any current indicator. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: Multiple flags active. (a) Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs represent a direct earnings headwind that could accelerate the $21.4B patent cliff impact in 2026-2027. (b) Post-COVID revenue normalization is still dragging EPS estimates lower. (c) Wiki records two prior passes at 35% confidence — this is the third consecutive pass on the same structural thesis. (d) RSI7=17.19 is extreme oversold but in a confirmed downtrend this is a distribution, not a buy signal without reversal confirmation. CONCLUSION: Macro regime is risk-on but pharma is not the rotation beneficiary here; energy and AI capex names are getting the flows. PFE's regulatory and fundamental headwinds are sector-specific and not offset by broad market strength. R/R below minimum bar. Pass.

XOM.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: The Iran military blockade of Iranian ports is the dominant macro catalyst directly benefiting XOM. Goldman raised Brent to $90/bbl on blockade risk; a Strait of Hormuz closure would spike oil further. This is a direct sector tailwind for integrated majors. The broader regime is risk-on (S&P ATH), which supports energy equities. Higher-for-longer rates are a mild headwind to valuation but secondary to the oil price catalyst. Iran escalation is both the thesis AND the exit trigger — if the blockade is resolved, exit immediately. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: XOM is recovering sharply after a 21d drawdown (-9.8% ROC 21d) driven by ceasefire-premium unwind. Near-term momentum has flipped: ROC 5d=+3.46%, ROC 10d=+3.80%. Hourly price is above ALL hourly EMAs (24/48/120) — full short-term alignment. Daily price is above EMA5, EMA10, EMA20, EMA21, EMA50; essentially at the SMA50 ($154.71). Hourly ADX=29.59 with plus_di=38.78 >> minus_di=21.49 confirms emerging bullish trend on intraday timeframe. IMPORTANT CONFLICT: Daily ADX minus_di (29.86) still slightly leads plus_di (22.10), and daily MACD is negative (-1.68), no crossover. The daily trend structure has not fully reversed; this is an early-phase recovery within a still-bearish daily structure. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry on a modest pullback to $153.00 (near hourly support at $151.34 is too deep). Entry $153.00, target $159.61 (next major resistance, ~4.3% upside), stop below $150.68 hourly pivot high (now support, ~1.5% downside from entry). Asymmetry: 4.3% / 1.5% = 2.9:1 — meets the minimum bar. If Iran escalates and oil spikes, Fib extension 1.272x at $157.83 is the intermediate target and $159.61 becomes achievable within 5-10 days. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: (a) Hourly OBV is diverging bearishly from price — price rising but OBV falling, suggesting the current hourly spike may not be fully institutionally backed. This is a caution flag on entry timing; a pullback to $153 would confirm stronger hands. (b) Hourly RSI=73.48 is overbought — entering at current levels risks catching the top of a short squeeze. (c) Wiki records three prior bearish calls (Apr 11-17) all on bounces; the macro has since shifted materially with blockade escalation, so regime continuity assumption is broken — current data wins. (d) 10-day volume bias is down on hourly — institutional activity is mixed. ENTRY DISCIPLINE: Do not chase at $154.33-$154.67; wait for a pullback toward $153 to improve R/R and confirm hourly OBV stabilizes. If no pullback, thesis requires Iran escalation headline to justify momentum entry.

PFE.reasoning: Branch A (Fade the Extreme) fires on the oversold side. RSI-7 at 17.19 is deeply below the 25 threshold — the strongest single trigger. Z-score at -1.94 is just shy of the -2 statistical threshold but close enough given the RSI confirmation. Price ($26.26) is testing the lower Bollinger Band (%B = 0.065), and the recent low of $26.20 represents a multi-test pivot support. Downside momentum accelerated (ROC -5.44% over 21 days) with volume running below average (0.88x), which is consistent with exhaustion rather than continuation. The hourly frame is already reversing: hourly RSI-21 at 47.94 (neutral-recovering), hourly MACD histogram turning positive (+0.031), and hourly OBV trending up — suggesting the snapback has started intraday. The hourly price ($26.70) is already above the daily close ($26.26), confirming early recovery. Confluence: daily pivot support at $26.36 (12 tests) and $26.68 (29 tests) sit just above current price. Wiki context shows prior runs had RSI at 49-51 with no setup — this is a new and genuine oversold condition, not a repeat false fade. Confidence is moderated because (1) the ADX at 25.48 shows the downtrend has some strength with -DI > +DI, (2) Z-score has not crossed -2, and (3) no confirmed daily reversal candle yet — the Apr 29 bar closed weak. Entry requires either a reversal candle on Apr 30 or a hold above $26.36. Target is the middle Bollinger Band / 50-SMA around $27.15-27.23. Stop below the recent low at $25.95.

XOM.reasoning: Branch A (Fade the Extreme — overbought) fires weakly, supported by Fibonacci confluence. RSI-7 at 79.95 is above the 75 overbought threshold after a sharp 2-day bounce (+4.4% from $148.19 to $154.67) on below-average volume (0.74x daily). The bounce has carried price directly into the 61.8% Fibonacci retracement level ($155.13, from swing high $176.41 to swing low $141.97), which also aligns with the well-tested resistance pivot at $155.89 — a strong confluence zone. Hourly OBV is declining and diverging from rising price (bearish divergence signal), and hourly RSI-21 at 73.48 is approaching overbought. The daily Z-score is essentially zero (-0.01), meaning price is exactly at the 50-day mean — this is the primary reason confidence is limited, as Branch A ideally wants Z-score > +2. However, the short-term RSI-7 extreme combined with the Fib/resistance confluence and OBV divergence provides a reasonable fade signal. Wiki context is a key factor: prior runs (Apr 11-17) identified this exact $151-155 zone as a short entry target; the bounce has now reached that level. Per strategy, repeat tests at the same resistance lower fresh-fade conviction. ADX on the daily shows -DI (29.86) > +DI (22.10), maintaining a slight downtrend bias. Target is the support cluster around $147.66-$148.00 (pivot low at $147.66, 20 tests). Stop above the resistance cluster at $156.50 (beyond $155.89 pivot high). Timeframe is short given this is a short-term RSI fade rather than a full Z-score extreme.

PFE.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 — approximately 4 trading days out. Net news sentiment: negative/mixed. EPS consensus implies ~19.6% YoY decline ($0.72-0.77), Metsera GLP-1 acquisition widely characterized as 'playing catch-up' vs. Lilly/Novo, FDA issued a misleading-ads warning on Adcetris, and the $21.4B patent cliff is a structural headwind. Partial offset: full-year guidance was reaffirmed ($2.80-$3.00 adj. EPS). Insider activity: CEO Bourla has been buying shares — a genuine bullish signal, but size undisclosed and insufficient to overcome the bearish news flow. Technical setup going in: poor. Daily price ($26.26) is below EMA-5 through EMA-50, MACD histogram deteriorating (-0.1247), ADX-DI configuration bearish (-DI 28.87 vs +DI 15.72), 5/10/21-day momentum all negative (ROC: -2.01%, -3.42%, -5.44%). RSI-7 at 17.19 is deeply oversold, creating a potential bounce risk, but oversold alone is not a constructive pre-catalyst setup — it is a downtrend. Hourly shows modest recovery (OBV trending up, relative volume 1.35x) but ADX hourly downtrend alignment is set. Pre-catalyst support at ~$26.20 (recent low) and $26.38 (hourly pivot). Resistance at $26.91 then $27.67. With the chart deteriorating into a binary print that already has a bearish consensus EPS read, there is no constructive technical base — the catalyst is approaching but the setup does not meet the entry criteria. Neutral: catalyst exists but setup is not constructive going in.

XOM.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 1, 2026 — approximately 1 trading day out. Net news sentiment: mixed with a complex offsetting structure. Bearish: U.S.-Iran ceasefire (Apr 10) collapsed the geopolitical risk premium (~14% Brent decline), Hormuz closure cut ~6% of Q1 global output, downstream timing headwinds of $3.3-5.3B, a $600-800M one-time impairment, and OPEC cutting Q2 demand forecast. Bullish: upstream earnings boosted ~$2.9B from elevated Q1 oil prices, CFO characterizes downstream timing effects as temporary and reversible, Uaru/Guyana production growth intact, $2B Pioneer synergies realized, lithium diversification launched. Analyst consensus is strong buy with average targets of $154-159 — essentially at current price — so even a beat may not provide meaningful upside surprise. Insider activity: none reported across all run history — quiet. Technical setup: daily chart constructive (price above short-term MAs, OBV up, RSI-14 neutral at 49.33, 5-day ROC +3.46%), but hourly shows warning flags: RSI-21 at 73.48 (overbought), OBV diverging bearishly from price, price at pct_b 0.8638 (near upper Bollinger), and immediate resistance at $155.89. The sharp 3-day recovery from $148 to $154.67 has already priced in some optimism ahead of earnings. With earnings in 1 trading day, a mixed upstream beat / downstream miss print, analyst targets already at current price, and hourly overbought conditions with OBV divergence, the risk/reward is too symmetric to take a directional swing position. Neutral: setup is borderline constructive but catalyst risk is too binary and ambiguous given the complex offsetting forces.
